story_text.value:
I am a Techstars mentor, former Chief Revenue Officer for a tech scale-up (now a unicorn), and most recently, founder of a startup I exited a few months ago. I started that venture from scratch, achieving seven figures in the first nine months with a junior team of three. Overall, I have 20 years of experience in tech sales.

Today, I'm searching for my soul and exploring what's next. Call me a recovering founder if you wish. I'm excited to do things I enjoy without focusing on their commercial aspects, instead seeking personal fulfillment. I'll think about money at some point, but it's not a priority right now.

So, I'd be happy to coach up to three tech startups, aiming to transfer as much knowledge as possible regarding sales management, revenue operations, and growth (excluding marketing specifics).

I'm a good fit if:

- Your startup has between 15-80 employees, or if you're bigger than that and still don't have a strong C-Level sales leader in place. The things we discuss will require effort on your part to implement, so you'll need resources.

- You're struggling with scaling sales, your sales process is all over the place, you don't understand how to get to the next step.

- Your product is tech/SaaS and vertical-specific.

- You're not a generic software development company

- You're B2B, and you sell to Enterprise or SMEs.

- You don't come from a sales background and don't have an experienced VP of Sales on your team. Or maybe you have a sales background, but your approach a bit rusty and you lost touch with modern sales and tools.

As much as I'd love to help if you're just starting out, my knowledge isn't an asset for solopreneurs or indie hackers.

The reasons why I'm doing this:

1. I'm soul-searching.

2. I'm exploring future new business ideas around sales consulting, and this exercise might give me some inspiration.

3. I want to reconnect with the founder community.

4. I genuinely f*king like it

If you're interested, please send me an email to "revenuecoaching@gmail.com" and include the below:

A) Company URL

B) N. of employees

C) ARR range (e.g. $200-500k, $500k-1M, More than $1M)

D) A line or two of what you're struggling with (e.g. I'm a tech CEO, I need sales guidance OR I'm a VP Sales but I need support scaling and with revenue ops OR we're an SMB and we feel our sales process is outdated OR I'm a CEO and my VP Sales just quit, etc.)

Point C is optional but, again, it will help me assess if I'm relevant to you or not, before we even get on a call. The topics I will advise you on will require resources and investment to translate into practice. If you're making less than $500k a year (or have raised less than $2-5M in VC money), you might find my help irrelevant to your stage.

I'll get back to you ASAP if I see a fit. If I don't, I'll let you know why.

title.value: I sold my startup, I'm now bored. I'll coach you on scaling sales for free

story_text.value:
Hey HN,

This is the post I’ve been waiting 5 months to write.

I launched my very first real SaaS product last month, a Chrome extension called ScreenSmooth that automatically adds smart zoom-ins and buttery-smooth cursor animations to any screen recording.

Why I built it:

Every single time I needed to record a product demo, tutorial, onboarding video, or bug report, I got frustrated. Loom, Screenity, Kap, etc. were all missing the one thing that makes videos actually look professional: automatic, natural-looking zooms + smooth cursor movement. I was spending hours editing in CapCut after every recording. So I decided to fix it myself.

The build journey (the real stuff):

- I Started January 2025 as a complete beginner with Chrome extension APIs.

- Learned canvas manipulation, WebRTC screen capture, mouse-path smoothing algorithms, and AI-based action detection from scratch.

- Biggest technical challenge: making the auto-zoom feel human instead of robotic. I rewrote the easing + timing logic 4 different times until it stopped making people motion-sick.

- Second biggest headache: keeping 1080p/60fps recordings smooth on low-end laptops without crashing the browser. Had to switch from one video processing library to another at 3 a.m. one night.

- Built everything locally for speed and privacy — no cloud upload required.

I shared every single step (the wins, the bugs, the “I’m about to quit” moments) transparently on X @Sayyidalijufri.

Then one day Marc Lou (300K+ followers) randomly bought a copy, loved it, and posted about it. That single tweet sent the whole thing viral in the indie hacker community.

The results (proof inside):

Here’s exactly what the revenue has looked like over the past 7 days:

$239 in 7 days

+405% vs previous period

100% profit margin (because it’s a simple Chrome extension)

All from organic social media. no ads. no sponsorships. Lifetime revenue now over $434 and still climbing.

What I learned as a first-time founder

1. People don’t buy features — they buy “I finally look professional without editing”.

2. Transparent building in public is 10x more powerful than any marketing hack.

3. One influential user (Marc) can change everything.

I’m still shipping almost daily — yesterday just shipped v0.4 with better cursor alignment and export quality.

Link: ScreenSmooth.com - Chrome Extension Screen Recorder with Auto Zoom

Thank you for being the amazing community on HackerNews — this journey wouldn’t have happened without the motivation I get from reading all your posts

title.value: My little SaaS made $239 in 7 days (Story)
